name: blue-hydra
title: blue-hydra
description:
Bluetooth device discovery service
BlueHydra is a Bluetooth device discovery service built on top of
the bluez library. BlueHydra makes use of ubertooth where available
and attempts to track both classic and low energy (LE) bluetooth devices
over time.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use blue_hydra because Bluetooth device discovery service.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use blue_hydra as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `blue_hydra [options] [args]` as documented upstream for blue_hydra.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair blue_hydra with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `blue_hydra --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
